title: Basics of East Asian Mahjong
summary: Irwin sought a comprehensive understanding of the rules and mechanics of East Asian Mahjong, specifically focusing on the widely played Chinese/Hong Kong variant. The conversation systematically covered the game's setup, detailing the roles of the East, South, West, and North players, before moving into fundamental moves like claiming a "Pong" (three identical tiles) or a "Chow." The discussion concluded by defining special tiles, including the eight "Flowers" (like Spring and Winter), and advanced plays such as "Kong," which involves claiming four identical tiles.
